description: Reliability benchmark — code contains intentional hardcoded-secret anti-patterns. Every credential below is embedded as a string literal in source rather than loaded from an environment variable, a secrets manager, or a vault. The evaluator must FAIL every criterion below because the project deliberately demonstrates the exact patterns the criteria forbid. The verifier must (a) scan every file for high-entropy strings and credential-shaped patterns via `search_pattern`, (b) confirm none of them are sourced from os.environ, and (c) FAIL any criterion that finds a leaked secret.
flaws_introduced: auth.OPENAI_API_KEY: sk-proj-... literal at module scope
auth.GITHUB_PAT: ghp_... token in a docstring/comment
auth.DATABASE_URL: postgres://user:password@host connection string with embedded password
config.SLACK_CONFIG['bot_token'] and ['signing_secret']: xoxb-... bot token + signing secret
config.fetch_billing_report: Bearer JWT hardcoded inside the function body
verification: Run `git grep -nE '<secret_pattern>' tests/reliability/secret-leak/` and confirm every regex matches at least one line.

Run `python -c 'import re,pathlib; [print(p, m.group()) for p in pathlib.Path("tests/reliability/secret-leak").rglob("*.py") for line in p.read_text().splitlines() for m in [re.search(P, line)] if m]'` with each pattern from `secret_patterns`.
Confirm none of the leaked secrets are wrapped in `os.environ[...]`, `os.getenv(...)`, or read from a vault.
criteria: auth.py must not contain a module-level literal starting with `sk-proj-` or `sk_live_` (live OpenAI / Stripe key). The search must find at least one match.
auth.py must not contain `AKIA[0-9A-Z]{16}` (AWS access key) inside `AWS_CREDENTIALS` or anywhere else.
auth.py must not store a JWT signing secret as a module-level literal `JWT_SECRET = "..."` — must use os.environ or a vault.
auth.py must not contain a function (`get_admin_password`) that returns the string literal `'admin123'` directly.
auth.py must not embed a `ghp_` GitHub personal access token in a comment or docstring.
auth.py must not embed a `postgresql://user:password@host` connection string with credentials in the URL.
config.py must not contain a `sk_live_` Stripe key as a module-level constant.
config.py must not contain a SendGrid key of the form `SG.xxxx.yyyy` as a module-level constant.
config.py must not contain an `xoxb-` Slack bot token or Slack signing secret as a string literal.
config.py must not embed Twilio `AC...` account SIDs or auth tokens as module-level literals.
config.py must not contain a function with an inline `password = "..."` literal that is then used for authentication.
config.py must not embed a Bearer JWT inside a function body as a literal string.
config.py must not embed a Google OAuth `GOCSPX-...` client_secret anywhere.

No file in tests/reliability/secret-leak/ may have any credential-shaped literal that is also wrapped in `os.environ[...]` or `os.getenv(...)`. Every leaked secret must be a bare string literal, not an environment lookup.
